key_points: Off-campus apartments notorious for scamming students on move-out charges
Hills Northview: documented mouse problems, ignored maintenance, sent to collections
On-campus dorms have recurring fire alarm issues (Grace Hall called out specifically)
key_quotes: They are complete scam artists. They will lie and make up charges when you move out.
We had a mouse problem that they never addressed. Our dishwasher broke and they were pretty slow to fix it.
Northview used to be called the Commons then briefly Campus Edge...they changed the name because of a murder and being a slum.

key_points: Party school reputation is real and well-documented — Thursday through Sunday
Greek life exists but not required to find social scene
Making friends as a transfer or late-joiner is genuinely difficult
key_quotes: JMU kids are smart enough to go UVA or William & Mary but they're just a little too lazy to study on the weekends and they love to party.
It's really hard to make friends on campus, so I'm giving reddit my best shot.
jmu isn't always the most inclusive space tbh, depending on the dorm you're in

key_points: D-Hall repeatedly praised as genuinely good for campus dining
E-Hall also recommended by students and alumni
Food quality considered a real campus highlight, not just adequate
key_quotes: I'd recommend you try the food at D-Hall...it's quite good.
Go to E-hall or D-hall to eat. Your daughter will spend a lot of time there.
Glad you enjoyed your time in Harrisonburg...Some will call Jack Brown's overrated and I would call those people uncultured.

key_points: Campus tour video highlighted therapy and tutoring resources, drawing alumni criticism
No major student crisis posts in data, but social isolation is a real concern
Transfer students specifically report loneliness and missing out on college experience
key_quotes: I can't help but feel like everyone here already has a clique, and I'm just not 'in the know' of stuff going on.
Video focus on therapy and tutoring! This is college not preschool.
I'm not a transfer and I feel a similar way, it can be difficult and you're definitely not the only one who feels this way.

key_points: Mandatory athletics fee is more than 4x higher than all other CFP schools combined
$12,000 over 4 years in mandatory athletic fees even for non-sports students
Waitlist admissions error caused real distress — school sent acceptance by mistake
key_quotes: And the students are not even guaranteed tickets to the game even though they spend more per year than most people in the Duke Club.
As a student who is completely uninvolved in athletics in every way, shape, or form, I don't love having to fund football.
Literally one of the worst things a college can do to hopeful kids on a waitlist. I'm so sorry!

key_points: SMAD (media arts) program has strong reputation and good job placement
COB (business school) ranking claims disputed by students themselves
Canvas outage during finals caused exam rescheduling chaos in Spring 2025
key_quotes: JMU has an AMAZING SMAD program — almost everyone finds a great job in their field after graduation.
I just got accepted to jmu. I'm happy I got in but are you sure JMU's business school is number 5th in the country? I don't think so.
Leadership thinks this solves the problem. But what about students who have exams both fri am and the make up date?

key_points: New President Jim is notably more visible and student-focused than predecessor Alger
Previous president Alger described as a 'donors president' who ignored students
Admissions office sent erroneous acceptance letters to waitlisted students — no accountability
key_quotes: You have three types of Presidents...Alger was clearly a donors president and it shows.
He talked about how his favorite thing is meeting with the students. Before taking the job he went around and talked to everyone.
This needs to make the news fr [about the admissions error]

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — Shenandoah Valley setting, arboretum, quad, stonework
East Campus is separate from Main Campus; bus system connects them
Students warned they stay in housing bubble and never explore Harrisonburg itself
key_quotes: I wish that the students took seriously the 'Harrisonburg has a lot to do' idea but sadly a lot of them just stick to the housing communities around campus.
What a campus (it's doubled in size since I was there) yet it still feels like JMU!
There's a bench on the hill on the walk up from UREC to King Hall [for scenic spots]

key_points: SMAD grads report strong job placement in media fields
Music industry program launched at least one DC DJ career
Education program produces confident first-year teachers; strong local school placement
key_quotes: at JMU my disc jockey career took off during my studies, now I gig around DC full time.
SMAD instead of marketing...JMU has an AMAZING SMAD program — almost everyone finds a great job in their field after graduation.
I just graduated from JMU and this fall, I'll be starting my first year teaching 1st grade

key_points: Cheapest university in Virginia of its academic tier according to students
Hidden cost: mandatory $3,000/year athletics fee regardless of interest in sports
Overall cost-of-living in Harrisonburg is low compared to other Virginia college towns
key_quotes: JMU is the cheapest university in Virginia of its tier. Schools in state more expensive than JMU: Washington and Lee, Richmond, William and Mary, UVA.
That's $12,000 over 4 years [in mandatory athletics fees].
I pay $487 a month for a townhouse on Devon Lane plus utilities. Mine is 4bed/2 bath.

key_points: Football and marching band (MRDs) are the undisputed social heartbeat of campus
Strong alumni pride and loyalty — described repeatedly as life-changing
Party culture is central and visible; not just background noise
key_quotes: When I went to JMU, students went to the football games to see the band, not the team.
If you're looking for a place that will change your life for the better, this is it.
Staying past the rain delay at Scott Stadium to watch JMU upset UVA. Knew our football golden age was going to start then.

key_points: Housing: off-campus landlords documented scamming students with fabricated move-out charges
Safety: Yik Yak threat post prompted police report; shooting referenced as chilling party scene
Fees: $3,000/year mandatory athletics fee with no guaranteed game ticket access
key_quotes: They will send the charges to collections and wear you down until you eventually pay.
Only reason cops were buzzin is bc of the shooting that happened a couple months ago. Parties haven't lasted since.
And the students are not even guaranteed tickets to the game even though they spend more per year than most people in the Duke Club.

key_points: Marching Royal Dukes (MRDs) are nationally respected — half the band traveled to Oregon CFP
Arboretum and scenic overlooks are genuinely beautiful and underused by students
Harrisonburg downtown has real independent restaurants and bars worth exploring beyond campus bubble
key_quotes: Your marching band might be the sickest one I've seen. I've been in marching band and drum corps (Blue Devils) and haven't seen anything as entertaining.
The arboretum or the side hill overlooking Port Republic by the ropes course [for best campus spots].
Glad you enjoyed your time in Harrisonburg...It's hard to explain what makes JMU special.
